mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates restrictive monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. When monthly payroll growth turns negative or falls below 50,000 jobs added, equity investors reduce risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 50,000 jobs for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market softening or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.15 in absolute value over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline month-over-month simultaneously, aggregate demand weakens, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 703,088 million dollars, HOUST at 1,277 thousand units
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal: sustained INDPRO growth produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved profit margins and reduced cost pressures.
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO months fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure labor market tightness. When UNRATE rises above 4.5% while PAYEMS growth decelerates below 75,000 jobs monthly, the labor market is cooling rapidly. This interaction signals reduced consumer income growth and negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 75,000 jobs
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or transition to recession
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE × PAYEMS growth) fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3.5% combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% indicates the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy to combat persistent price pressures. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days in high-inflation, high-rates regimes due to sustained real rate pressure on valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both CPIAUCSL > 3.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as an always-on trend signal. When annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, aggregate demand is expanding sufficiently to support corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days through improved profit expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 22,758.752 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarters with annualized GDPC1 growth > 2.5% fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, both supply and labor market deterioration signal stagflationary pressure. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward amid supply constraints and labor weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises month-over-month in the same month
regime_hint: stagflationary environment or supply shock with labor market softening
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.
